content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - will modify outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, add create_jinja_env helper). Will check back before finalizing.

agent1: my feature adds per-decorator custom filters via a 'filters' param on @prompt/from_str/from_file, plus a create_jinja_env() helper that centralizes env creation + filter registration. This should be complementary to your global register_filter/unregister_filter - create_jinja_env can apply the global registry first, then overlay per-call filters. I'll implement now; ping me before you touch _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt so we don't clobber signatures - happy to sync on the final function signatures.

agent1: heads up - we appear to be in separate workspace copies (your GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY edits aren't showing up in my git diff). So there's no literal file clobbering risk - we each submit our own patch.txt independently. My task spec only calls for the 'filters' param + create_jinja_env() helper (no global registry), so I'll keep my create_jinja_env() clean per that spec and not add GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY - that's your feature to own. If the evaluator merges both patches, create_jinja_env would just need your registry.update() line added back in, but that's a merge-time concern, not something I can/should do from my sandbox. Good luck!
